---
title: "YouTube Trending Videos Report"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: false
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(scales)

paths <- list(
  growth_weekly = "../outputs/tables/category_growth_weekly.csv",
  growth_summary = "../outputs/tables/category_growth_summary.csv",
  engagement_summary = "../outputs/tables/engagement_summary.csv",
  outliers = "../outputs/tables/engagement_outliers.csv",
  scatter = "../outputs/figures/engagement_scatter.png",
  heatmap = "../outputs/figures/category_engagement_heatmap.png",
  rank_gif = "../outputs/figures/category_rank_animation.gif"
)

missing_items <- names(paths)[!file.exists(unlist(paths))]
if (length(missing_items) > 0) {
  stop(
    paste0(
      "Missing required outputs: ",
      paste(missing_items, collapse = ", "),
      ". Run source('run_project.R') first."
    )
  )
}

growth_weekly <- readr::read_csv(paths$growth_weekly, show_col_types = FALSE)
growth_summary <- readr::read_csv(paths$growth_summary, show_col_types = FALSE)
engagement_summary <- readr::read_csv(paths$engagement_summary, show_col_types = FALSE)
outliers <- readr::read_csv(paths$outliers, show_col_types = FALSE)

safe_num <- function(x) ifelse(is.finite(x), x, NA_real_)

growth_summary <- growth_summary %>%
  mutate(
    median_growth_rate = safe_num(median_growth_rate),
    max_growth_rate = safe_num(max_growth_rate),
    min_growth_rate = safe_num(min_growth_rate)
  )

key_top_volume <- growth_summary %>%
  slice_max(order_by = avg_weekly_count, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

key_fastest_growth <- growth_summary %>%
  filter(!is.na(median_growth_rate)) %>%
  slice_max(order_by = median_growth_rate, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

key_highest_engagement <- engagement_summary %>%
  slice_max(order_by = avg_engagement_rate, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE)

outlier_count <- nrow(outliers)
outlier_share <- if (sum(engagement_summary$videos, na.rm = TRUE) > 0) {
  outlier_count / sum(engagement_summary$videos, na.rm = TRUE)
} else {
  NA_real_
}

render_nice_table <- function(df, caption = NULL) {
  knitr::kable(
    df,
    format = "html",
    caption = caption,
    table.attr = "class='table table-striped table-hover table-bordered table-sm align-middle report-table'"
  )
}
```

<style>
.report-table {
  font-size: 0.93rem;
  border-radius: 10px;
  overflow: hidden;
  box-shadow: 0 1px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06);
}
.report-table thead th {
  background-color: #f4f7fb;
  font-weight: 700;
  border-bottom: 2px solid #d9e2ef;
}
.report-table tbody tr:hover {
  background-color: #f8fbff;
}
caption {
  caption-side: top;
  text-align: left;
  font-weight: 600;
  color: #1f3b57;
  margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
}
</style>

## Executive Summary

- Most consistently dominant category by weekly trending volume: **`r key_top_volume$category_id`**
  with ~**`r comma(round(key_top_volume$avg_weekly_count, 0))`** videos/week.
- Fastest median week-over-week growth category: **`r key_fastest_growth$category_id`**
  with median growth of **`r percent(key_fastest_growth$median_growth_rate, accuracy = 0.1)`**.
- Highest average engagement category: **`r key_highest_engagement$category_id`**
  at **`r percent(key_highest_engagement$avg_engagement_rate, accuracy = 0.01)`** engagement.
- Engagement outliers detected: **`r comma(outlier_count)`**
  (`r percent(outlier_share, accuracy = 0.01)` of observations).

## Visuals

### 1) Animated Category Rank Chart

![](../outputs/figures/category_rank_animation.gif){fig-alt="Animated weekly rank chart of top categories"}

### 2) Engagement Scatter

![](../outputs/figures/engagement_scatter.png){fig-alt="Scatter plot of engagement rate versus views"}

### 3) Category Heatmap

![](../outputs/figures/category_engagement_heatmap.png){fig-alt="Heatmap of average engagement by weekday and category"}

## Category Growth

```{r}
growth_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_weekly_count)) %>%
  mutate(
    avg_weekly_count = round(avg_weekly_count, 2),
    median_growth_rate = percent(median_growth_rate, accuracy = 0.1),
    max_growth_rate = percent(max_growth_rate, accuracy = 0.1),
    min_growth_rate = percent(min_growth_rate, accuracy = 0.1)
  ) %>%
  head(15) %>%
  render_nice_table("Top 15 Categories by Average Weekly Trending Volume")
```

## Engagement Analytics

```{r}
engagement_summary %>%
  arrange(desc(avg_engagement_rate)) %>%
  mutate(
    median_views = comma(round(median_views, 0)),
    avg_engagement_rate = percent(avg_engagement_rate, accuracy = 0.01),
    p95_engagement_rate = percent(p95_engagement_rate, accuracy = 0.01)
  ) %>%
  head(15) %>%
  render_nice_table("Top 15 Categories by Average Engagement Rate")
```

## Outlier Snapshot

```{r}
outliers %>%
  mutate(engagement_rate = percent(engagement_rate, accuracy = 0.01)) %>%
  select(trending_date, region, category_id, title, channel_title, views, engagement_rate, z_engagement) %>%
  head(20) %>%
  render_nice_table("Sample of Engagement Outliers")
```
